text.title: OpenAPI Authoring Checklist
text.summary: Concrete checklist for every OpenAPI spec: required fields, versioning, error responses, linting, and CI gates.

rule.statement: Each operation must have: operationId (camelCase), summary (imperative phrase), description (optional but useful), tags (1+), requestBody examples, and refs to all possible error responses.
rule.rationale: Codegen uses operationId; missing causes ugly method names. Error refs teach SDK consumers what to expect.

rule.statement: Common error responses (400 BadRequest, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 NotFound, 429 TooManyRequests, 422 ValidationError) must live in components/responses and be referenced, never inlined.
rule.rationale: One canonical error shape per code prevents clients handling two different error bodies for the same status.

rule.statement: Set additionalProperties: false on all request schemas and additionalProperties: true only where intentionally extensible on response schemas.
rule.rationale: additionalProperties defaults to true, silently allowing unknown fields that later strict validation breaks.

rule.statement: For polymorphism, use oneOf with discriminator.propertyName mapping all variants. Never use anyOf for discriminated unions.
rule.rationale: anyOf without discriminator causes generators to emit unsafe union types; runtime parsing breaks.

rule.statement: In OpenAPI 3.1, use type: [string, "null"] for nullable fields, not nullable: true.
rule.rationale: nullable: true is a 3.0 extension; 3.1 follows JSON Schema 2020-12 where null is a type.

rule.statement: Run spectral lint, redocly lint, and oasdiff breaking check on every PR against the main branch spec.
rule.rationale: Without oasdiff, breaking changes ship to SDK consumers undetected.

rule.statement: Run schemathesis against a running impl on every PR to detect spec-vs-impl drift.
rule.rationale: A spec that validates but whose server emits a different shape is worse than no spec — clients trust the spec.

antipattern.description: Spec exists as docs only — no mocks, no codegen, no contract tests.
antipattern.reason: Spec becomes stale; code diverges; clients break silently on next release.

antipattern.description: Hand-editing the bundled openapi.yaml instead of source shards.
antipattern.reason: Next bundle run overwrites edits; agents lose changes.
SDK users and LLMs learn the API shape from examples; missing examples = more support questions + hallucinated payloads.
